articles.title: India's power demand to rise by 5 to 5.5 percent in FY27: ICRA
articles.description: India's power demand is set for a significant rise of 5.0-5.5 percent in 2026-27. This growth will be fueled by industrial, commercial, agricultural, and household sectors. Emerging sources like electric vehicles and data centers will also contribute. Thermal plant utilization will stay steady. Distribution companies face challenges with high debt and muted tariff hikes, impacting their profitability.

articles.title: Oil Cos Needs To Re-Engage With Upstream Expansion Abroad: S&P Global
articles.description: India's energy security I heavily dependent on imports and the West Asia crisis has laid bare India's fragile Oil & Gas sector. In the backdrop of the ongoing crisis, S&P Global's Rajeev Lala and Gauri Jauhar share their perspective on Why India's National Oil Companies should relook at investing in E&P opportunities abroad, What could've been done to secure operational and strategic reserves? Listen In

articles.title: Global markets: AI and chip boom fuel expansion of trillion-dollar club
articles.description: Currently, 13 companies worldwide are valued at more than $1 trillion. Of these, 10 are from the US, with Taiwan, South Korea, and Saudi Arabia contributing one each. An additional 13 companies globally sit at the half-trillion-dollar mark, nine of them American, and one each from China, the Netherlands, South Korea, and Mexico.

articles.title: Vendors accuse Bhooth Bangla makers of Rs 30 lakh non-payment; Balaji denies allegations
articles.description: Bhooth Bangla has landed in controversy after vendors accused the makers of unpaid dues, including an alleged pending payment of Rs 30 lakh. The film continues to perform strongly at the ticket window.

articles.description: The all-India thermal plant load factor (PLF or capacity utilisation) level fell to 65-66 per cent in 2025-26 amid demand moderation and is likely to remain around 65 per cent in 2026-27

articles.title: Vedanta dividend: How the mega demerger will impact payout for 21 lakh shareholders?
articles.description: Vedanta's mega demerger has spun off four new companies, impacting dividend payouts for its 21 lakh shareholders. While the parent company is expected to remain dividend-paying, its absolute dividend per share will likely decline. Investors will now need to assess each demerged entity's cash flow and growth plans for future income.

articles.title: India shielded fuel supply through crude diversification amid Hormuz turmoil: S&P Global Energy official
articles.description: India's refinery operations remain robust despite global energy market disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The nation has diversified crude purchases, increasing imports from multiple sources, including significant Russian oil acquisition, to maintain domestic fuel supplies. While India's energy system faces pressure, its efficient buying strategy and diversified sourcing have kept refineries running at strong levels.

articles.title: Rs 2,170 crore in gross bookings, Rs 24 crore EBITDA: So why is Yatra stock still down?

With corporate travel, higher-margin segments, and platform expansion now driving strategy, the market is no longer asking whether Yatra can grow, but whether it can deliver consistent, predictable earnings over time.
